Welcome to Thinker Onboarding

You've been selected as a Divine Thinker. Your cognitive framework will be used to give world-class perspective to founders making their toughest decisions.

What you'll set up:
✦ Your public identity & title
✦ Your cognitive framework (how you think)
✦ Your lens statement (your signature view)
✦ Your core decision-making domains

Your identity

This is how you appear to founders seeking perspective.

Keep it short. This appears under your name in the thinker picker.

Your cognitive framework

This is the core of your Thinker model — how you process decisions, what you look for, what you challenge, and how you communicate truth.

Be specific. The more precise your framework, the sharper your AI model's output.

Your lens statement

One sentence that captures how you uniquely see the world. This appears on your public thinker card.

Write in third person. It should describe what a founder gets from your perspective.

Your core domains

What kinds of decisions are you best equipped to advise on? Add the areas where your framework creates the most value.

Add 3–8 domains. These help founders find the right thinker for their decision.
✦

You're live as a Thinker

Your profile and model are active. Founders can now request your perspective.

Your public Thinker Card
This is exactly how founders see you when browsing the Thinkers directory.
Update your lens and domains in the Train Model tab to refine it.
Cognitive Moat Score — How it's calculated
Your Cognitive Moat Score measures how defensible and distinct your expertise signal is. It ranges from 0–100 and is computed across five dimensions.
How to improve your score
1. Sharpen your Theory of Victory — the more specific, the higher your Framework Depth score.
2. Add 5–10 domains to your constellation to improve Domain Coverage.
3. Accumulate sessions — each perspective served raises your Signal Strength.
4. Write a precise Lens statement — one clear sentence outperforms vague generalism.
Sample perspective output
This is a representative example of how your model responds to a founder's question. It uses your current framework and lens — publish to make it live.
This preview is generated from your saved framework — it costs nothing to preview.
Divine Intelligence

The operating system for founders who run on data.

Divine automates your growth protocols, audits team performance against real benchmarks, and delivers live financial transparency directly to your investors — no decks, no delays.

1
Automate growth protocols
Sync your QuickBooks financials, authorize your Council of Thinkers, and deliver live transparency to your investors — from a single Terminal.
2
Audit team performance
Run your numbers against the five diligence triggers that investors actually use. Know where you stand before they ask.
3
Brief your investors in real time
Grant sovereign access to your live financial ledger. Your board never has to ask for an update again.
Build your intelligence council.

You're the Founder. Invite your team, advisors, investors, and staff — each with sovereign access to exactly what they need to see.

Thinker Lab
Install the Council of Thinkers. Live financial audits, structured decision protocols, and institutional-grade strategy.
Advisory Board
Authorize domain experts across Marketing, Product, Sales. They access verified Perspectivess — not your finances.
Investor Hub
Live data room. Real-time financial transparency for your investors — no decks, no delays.
Staff Terminal
Delegate tasks with clear Success Protocols. Everyone knows exactly what "done" looks like.
How Perspectives work
What do I get per perspective?
A structured perspective through a specific Thinker's cognitive framework — key insight plus analytical frames applied to your exact situation.
How specific should my question be?
Specific is better. "Should I pivot?" produces a generic answer. "Should I pivot from B2C to B2B given our 12% MoM churn?" produces a sharp one.
Can I try multiple Thinkers?
Yes. After receiving a Perspective, you can try the same question through a different Thinker to surface contrasting views.
Where are my past Perspectives saved?
Every Perspective is saved to your session history in the sidebar. Sign in to persist history across devices.

The more precise your framing, the sharper the perspective.
